summary: The batch of Amharic words underwent an 8-protocol scholarly analysis. The SPLITTER protocol was applied to 'መጐዳቱንና', 'ከአልዙሰር', 'ከብዙዎችም', 'ለአጥቂዎቹ', 'በኀጢአተኛው', and 'የድህነታችንም', resulting in their decomposition into constituent words and affixes. The JUDGE protocol found all words to be valid Amharic or recognizable loanwords/proper nouns, with no pure gibberish or unfixable OCR errors. The CORRECTOR protocol stripped any attached punctuation and addressed minor visual confusions. The LEMMATIZER protocol identified the base dictionary forms for all words, such as 'መረስ' for 'እየረሱት' and 'ማደግ' for 'ላላደገች'. The TRANSLATOR protocol provided comprehensive English synonyms. The GRAMMARIAN protocol assigned appropriate Parts of Speech, noting instances where participles functioned as adjectives or nouns. The FILTER protocol ensured all words and roots were at least two characters long. Scholarly Guesses were made for 'ቦረር' (assumed as a noun/place name) and 'ሄሉያ' (treated as a proper noun/specific term) due to lack of contextual disambiguation. The final output is a structured JSON dataset.

The input batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting based on morphological transitions, such as 'እንደተዋሃድንለፍቅራችን' into 'እንደተዋሃድን' and 'ለፍቅራችን', and 'ሲልከናፍራችን' into 'ሲል' and 'ከናፍራችን'. For words like 'በሚካኤልና', 'ከአዋጅና', and 'ፍላሚንጎና', the trailing 'ና' (meaning 'and') was treated as a conjunction suffix rather than a separate word. This 'Scholarly Guess' was made to prevent the 'ና' from being discarded by Protocol 7 (Size Constraint) as a single-character word, which would have altered the intended meaning. The root for these words was derived from the main noun (e.g., 'ሚካኤል' for 'በሚካኤልና'). 'ዲለሆስ' was identified as a proper noun and retained, assuming it's a name or specific term, as it did not constitute gibberish. All other words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and tagged according to their linguistic properties, ensuring adherence to the size constraint.

The input batch underwent rigorous processing according to the 8 scholarly protocols. Several words were identified as merged and were split into their constituent parts: 'ለፊቱንና' into 'ለፊቱን' and 'እና'; 'የከረንቡላና' into 'የከረንቡላ' and 'እና'; 'ከኮምቦልቻና' into 'ከኮምቦልቻ' and 'እና'; 'መታመማቸውና' into 'መታመማቸው' and 'እና'; 'የማሠባሠብና' into 'የማሰባሰብ' and 'እና'; and 'ያሰፈነና' into 'ያሰፈነ' and 'እና'. Two words, 'ባድንስ' and 'ቦልስቲክ', were discarded as they are direct transliterations of foreign words ('badminton' and 'ballistic' respectively) and lack a native Amharic root or functional semantic value for a general Amharic dictionary. Visual OCR corrections were applied to 'ሀጢአቶቼን' which was corrected to 'ኃጢአቶቼን' (a common 'ሀ'/'ሃ' confusion) and 'የማሠባሠብና' to 'የማሰባሰብና' (a common 'ሠ'/'ሰ' confusion). Scholarly guesses were made in lemmatization for words like 'ከመቅዝበት', 'የማያውቀቱን', 'አሟሟታችንስ', and 'ሽልማቱማ', where grammatical suffixes or enclitics ('በት', 'ን', 'ስ', 'ማ') were recognized as part of the word's inflection rather than separate dictionary entries, thus extracting the core root ('መቅዘፍ', 'ማወቅ', 'አሟሟት', 'ሽልማት' respectively). All roots and words included are at least two characters long, adhering to the size constraint. Lemmatization focused on identifying the true infinitive or base form, such as 'ማራባት' for 'የሚያራባው' and 'ማክበር' for 'ሲያከብሩበት', ensuring accurate dictionary entries. Parts of speech were assigned to major categories only.

The input batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. No words required splitting. The word 'ዴህጋን' was discarded as it appears to be a foreign proper noun or a low-value term for a general Amharic dictionary. Visual OCR corrections were applied to 'በበሀይሉ' (corrected to 'በኃይሉ'), 'ሳፀዳ' (corrected to 'ሳጸዳ'), and 'ለቢዮሎዢ' (corrected to 'ለባዮሎጂ'). A scholarly guess was made for 'የዋች', correcting it to 'የዋህ' based on common OCR errors and the high frequency of 'የዋህ' in Amharic. All remaining words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and tagged with their major part of speech. All included words are at least two characters long.

summary: The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. No words were discarded as gibberish or unfixable. Several words required visual OCR correction, specifically 'ማኅበረሰበችን' to 'ማኅበረሰባችን' (correcting the vowel for possessive suffix), 'ጦረኝነነት' to 'ጦረኝነት' (removing a redundant 'ነ'), 'ከመጐርበጥ' to 'ከመጎርበጥ' (correcting 'ጐ' to 'ጎ'), 'በከዚራ' to 'በከዘራ' (correcting 'ዚ' to 'ዘ'), and 'ከፃፍኩም' to 'ከጻፍኩም' (correcting 'ፃ' to 'ጻ'). The word 'ራፖርም' was cleaned to 'ራፖርም' as the 'ም' suffix is valid for 'also'. Lemmatization focused on identifying the base infinitive or dictionary form. Part of Speech tagging considered the morphological structure, including gerundial forms as Nouns and relative clauses as Verbs or Adjectives. All words met the minimum two-character length requirement.

summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required visual repair (e.g., 'ቤተከርስቲያን' to 'ቤተክርስቲያን', 'እንድስታስተናግድ' to 'እንድታስተናግድ', 'አድረጊ' to 'አድርጊ', 'ሚረዳው' to 'የሚረዳው', 'ነውረ' to 'ነውር') due to common OCR errors or informal spellings. Merged words were identified and split, specifically 'ፈረንሳዊውአሌክሲስ' into 'ፈረንሳዊው' and 'አሌክሲስ', 'በዘቀጠና' into 'በዘቀጠ' and 'እና' (scholarly guess for a likely merged conjunction or OCR error), and 'ዘምዘምና' into 'ዘምዘም' and 'እና'. All words were successfully lemmatized to their global citation forms and assigned appropriate parts of speech and comprehensive English synonyms. No words were discarded based on the 'JUDGE' or 'FILTER' protocols, as all were fixable Amharic words of sufficient length.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words were identified as valid Amharic and underwent lemmatization, translation, and POS tagging. 'ሃሽሊ', 'ገገባር', 'ሞኮሚቲ', and 'ፍራንሴና' were discarded as they appear to be foreign names, transliterations, or unfixable gibberish without clear Amharic roots. 'በፕሮፕራይድ' was split into 'በ' and 'ፕሮፕራይድ'; both were discarded ('በ' for being a single character, 'ፕሮፕራይድ' for being a foreign transliteration). Similarly, 'የዘቢደር' was split into 'የ' and 'ዘቢደር', and both were discarded for the same reasons. All remaining words met the minimum length requirement of two characters.

summary: The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. No words required splitting (PROTOCOL 1). All words were deemed valid Amharic or proper nouns (PROTOCOL 2). Several OCR corrections were made under PROTOCOL 3: 'ማህበረሠባዊና' was corrected to 'ማህበረሰባዊና' (ሠ to ሰ), 'ዝግጅዎች' to 'ዝግጅቶች' (plural suffix correction), 'ስለሔዱና' to 'ስለሄዱና' (ሔ to ሄ), 'እዱር' to 'እድር' (ዱ to ድ), and 'ጀምሬአሁና' to 'ጀምሬያለሁና' (አሁ to ያለሁ). Lemmatization (PROTOCOL 4) was applied to derive the infinitive or base form for each word. Comprehensive English synonyms (PROTOCOL 5) and Part of Speech tags (PROTOCOL 6) were assigned. All words met the minimum length requirement (PROTOCOL 7). Proper nouns like 'እንሲፋኖስ' and 'ሞቪስታር' were treated as their own roots.

summary: The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting due to merged suffixes like 'ና' (and) and 'ም' (also/even), such as 'የሚነቃውና' into 'የሚነቃው' and 'እና', 'እንደነበሩበትና' into 'እንደነበሩበት' and 'እና', 'በተወውም' into 'በተወው' and 'ም', 'እንዲወሩና' into 'እንዲወሩ' and 'እና', 'ካራቺና' into 'ካራቺ' and 'እና', 'ዝለታቸውም' into 'ዝለታቸው' and 'ም', 'እንደሌለብንም' into 'እንደሌለብን' and 'ም', and 'እንዲያንስና' into 'እንዲያንስ' and 'እና'. All identified words were valid Amharic and passed the 'Judge' protocol. Punctuation was stripped where applicable. Lemmatization focused on identifying the infinitive form for verbs and the base noun for nominal forms, e.g., 'ካነሱላቸው' to 'ማንሳት' and 'አለመንከባከብን' to 'መንከባከብ'. Proper nouns like 'ጂዶ', 'ሒልተን', 'ካራቺ', and 'ፊዦ' were retained as their own roots. All words met the minimum two-character length requirement of the 'Filter' protocol. Synonyms and Parts of Speech were assigned based on the lemmatized forms and contextual understanding.

The batch of Amharic words was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. Most words were straightforward in their cleaning, lemmatization, and tagging. One word, 'ወርቃለማሁስ', was identified as a merged string and was split into 'ወርቅ' (gold) and 'አለማሁስ' (if I didn't cultivate/make). This split was based on morphological analysis, recognizing 'ወርቅ' as a distinct noun and 'አለማሁስ' as a verb phrase with a negative past tense and a conditional/causal suffix. No words were discarded due to gibberish, unfixable errors, or being single characters. All words successfully passed the size constraint filter. Scholarly guesses were made in determining the most appropriate root for words with multiple possible derivations (e.g., 'ቀዳቻቸውና' from 'ቀዳዳ') and in providing comprehensive synonyms to capture the full semantic range.
